text.content: How to best maintain your construction equipment tires

rich_text.plain_text: How to best maintain your construction equipment tires

text.content: In addition to maintaining your construction equipment, maintaining your construction machine’s tires is equally important to run an efficient operation. By maintaining your construction tires, it’ll have a direct impact on your bottom line with the following attributes:

rich_text.plain_text: In addition to maintaining your construction equipment, maintaining your construction machine’s tires is equally important to run an efficient operation. By maintaining your construction tires, it’ll have a direct impact on your bottom line with the following attributes:

text.content: Minimize repair cost.

rich_text.plain_text: Minimize repair cost.

text.content: Improve tire life and overall operation safety.

rich_text.plain_text: Improve tire life and overall operation safety.

text.content: Maximize operation’s productivity.

rich_text.plain_text: Maximize operation’s productivity.

text.content: Minimize downtime.

rich_text.plain_text: Minimize downtime.

text.content: Potentially reduce fuel consumption.

rich_text.plain_text: Potentially reduce fuel consumption.

text.content: Here are five factors to consider when it comes to construction equipment tire maintenance:

rich_text.plain_text: Here are five factors to consider when it comes to construction equipment tire maintenance:

rich_text.plain_text: Air pressure

rich_text.plain_text: Picking the right application

rich_text.plain_text: Avoid weather hazards

text.content: I. Air pressure

rich_text.plain_text: I. Air pressure

text.content: One of the most important factors on maintaining your construction tires, bias, or radial, is air pressure. Always check your tires’ air pressure daily, ideally before each shift, especially on a “cold” tire. Below is how a “cold” tire versus a “hot” tire is defined:

rich_text.plain_text: One of the most important factors on maintaining your construction tires, bias, or radial, is air pressure. Always check your tires’ air pressure daily, ideally before each shift, especially on a “cold” tire. Below is how a “cold” tire versus a “hot” tire is defined:

text.content: [table content omitted]

rich_text.plain_text: [table content omitted]

text.content:
If you have an underinflated tire, you risk the potential of heat buildup, leading damage to the sidewall, bead and lining. A drop of simply 5 psi can lead to 10% drop in load carrying capacity, and a drop of 10 psi can lead to 10% drop in overall tire life.

Similarly, if you have an overinflated tire, it leaves your tire being vulnerable to impact damage as additional pressure can stress the tire casing.

In general,  failure to maintain proper air pressure will cause the tire to:

rich_text.plain_text:
If you have an underinflated tire, you risk the potential of heat buildup, leading damage to the sidewall, bead and lining. A drop of simply 5 psi can lead to 10% drop in load carrying capacity, and a drop of 10 psi can lead to 10% drop in overall tire life.

Similarly, if you have an overinflated tire, it leaves your tire being vulnerable to impact damage as additional pressure can stress the tire casing.

In general,  failure to maintain proper air pressure will cause the tire to:

text.content: Have excessive wear

rich_text.plain_text: Have excessive wear

text.content: Tire component separation

rich_text.plain_text: Tire component separation

text.content: Tire rim slip

rich_text.plain_text: Tire rim slip

text.content: Reduced tire life and productivity

rich_text.plain_text: Reduced tire life and productivity

text.content:
In addition to air pressure, always remember that if you need to increase load, you must increase the air inflation pressure to match the tire to the load. Maxam Tire provides the exact speed symbol, load carrying capacity and the proper psi that correlates to each tire on our product catalog. Always refer to this information when you need to increase air pressure based on load carrying capacity.

Never run excess load continuously on your construction machine for too long, even if it’s based on the proper carrying load and speed, because it’ll shorten your tires’ performance and overall life significantly.

rich_text.plain_text:
In addition to air pressure, always remember that if you need to increase load, you must increase the air inflation pressure to match the tire to the load. Maxam Tire provides the exact speed symbol, load carrying capacity and the proper psi that correlates to each tire on our product catalog. Always refer to this information when you need to increase air pressure based on load carrying capacity.

Never run excess load continuously on your construction machine for too long, even if it’s based on the proper carrying load and speed, because it’ll shorten your tires’ performance and overall life significantly.

text.content: II. Picking the right application

rich_text.plain_text: II. Picking the right application

text.content:
Having the right type of tire for your application is equally important as maintaining proper air pressure. Contractors should always consider the machine type, what type of jobs that machine does most of the time and pick the right tire accordingly.

Below are two cases on backhoe and how each backhoe’s job determines what tires to pick.

rich_text.plain_text:
Having the right type of tire for your application is equally important as maintaining proper air pressure. Contractors should always consider the machine type, what type of jobs that machine does most of the time and pick the right tire accordingly.

Below are two cases on backhoe and how each backhoe’s job determines what tires to pick.

text.content: [table content omitted]

rich_text.plain_text: [table content omitted]

text.content: Sometimes having a rubber track for your application could be the ultimate solution. For example, landscape crew moving rocks on a skid steer should consider having rubber tracks instead of tires. This is due to the importance of soil compaction, by carrying the same amount of load on a rubber track, you can disperse that load with the greater contact area, providing least amount of compaction on the lawn and the roots.

rich_text.plain_text: Sometimes having a rubber track for your application could be the ultimate solution. For example, landscape crew moving rocks on a skid steer should consider having rubber tracks instead of tires. This is due to the importance of soil compaction, by carrying the same amount of load on a rubber track, you can disperse that load with the greater contact area, providing least amount of compaction on the lawn and the roots.

text.content: III. Avoid weather hazards

rich_text.plain_text: III. Avoid weather hazards

text.content: Always inspect critical areas on your tires before the changing season, areas to inspect on your tires include:

rich_text.plain_text: Always inspect critical areas on your tires before the changing season, areas to inspect on your tires include:

text.content: Valve stem: any issues at the bead and rims, anything bent?

rich_text.plain_text: Valve stem: any issues at the bead and rims, anything bent?

text.content: Bead and rims: are the valve stems good and tight; do you have valve caps, cords leaking?

rich_text.plain_text: Bead and rims: are the valve stems good and tight; do you have valve caps, cords leaking?

text.content: Sidewall: Sidewall: fatigue cracking inside and out? Inspect the inside and look for any bubbles, golf balls sticking out (indication of impact damage).

rich_text.plain_text: Sidewall: Sidewall: fatigue cracking inside and out? Inspect the inside and look for any bubbles, golf balls sticking out (indication of impact damage).

text.content: Face of the tire: steel belting, bulge, lug tearing off?

rich_text.plain_text: Face of the tire: steel belting, bulge, lug tearing off?

text.content: After your tire inspection, avoid the following operation despite of weather hazards:

rich_text.plain_text: After your tire inspection, avoid the following operation despite of weather hazards:

text.content: Spinning tires excessively: don’t push too hard as it’ll create heat buildup, which is ultimately can shorten tire life.

rich_text.plain_text: Spinning tires excessively: don’t push too hard as it’ll create heat buildup, which is ultimately can shorten tire life.

text.content: Avoid puddles: water is a lubricant on rubber, if something sharp is hidden, running over them could cut the tires.

rich_text.plain_text: Avoid puddles: water is a lubricant on rubber, if something sharp is hidden, running over them could cut the tires.

text.content: Slow down: excess speed will increase heat buildup (remember your carrying capacity).

rich_text.plain_text: Slow down: excess speed will increase heat buildup (remember your carrying capacity).

text.content: IV. Pre-shift Tire Check

rich_text.plain_text: IV. Pre-shift Tire Check

text.content:
Tires should be visually inspected before every shift for cuts and impacts that could lead to tire failure. Routine maintenance inspections can lead to discovering deeper cuts that have exposed plies. In these cases, removing the tire and repairing it immediately can extend the life of the tire, increasing site’s profitability.

Below are the basic components on the tires you should always inspect before each shift daily:

rich_text.plain_text:
Tires should be visually inspected before every shift for cuts and impacts that could lead to tire failure. Routine maintenance inspections can lead to discovering deeper cuts that have exposed plies. In these cases, removing the tire and repairing it immediately can extend the life of the tire, increasing site’s profitability.

Below are the basic components on the tires you should always inspect before each shift daily:

text.content: Check the face of the tire

rich_text.plain_text: Check the face of the tire

text.content: Check for cuts and tears on the face of the tire.  Is anything missing?  Has the lug torn off?

rich_text.plain_text: Check for cuts and tears on the face of the tire.  Is anything missing?  Has the lug torn off?

text.content: Check the bead

rich_text.plain_text: Check the bead

text.content: Run your hand all around where the tire meets the wheel.  Is it consistent all the way around.

rich_text.plain_text: Run your hand all around where the tire meets the wheel.  Is it consistent all the way around.

text.content: Check the valve stem

rich_text.plain_text: Check the valve stem

text.content: Check the core.  Is it leaking?  Is there a cap on the valve stem?  Is it tight?  Is the valve stem properly seated on the wheel? All of this can be checked by spraying a little bit of soapy water on the stem.  If you see bubbles, you may have a leak.

rich_text.plain_text: Check the core.  Is it leaking?  Is there a cap on the valve stem?  Is it tight?  Is the valve stem properly seated on the wheel? All of this can be checked by spraying a little bit of soapy water on the stem.  If you see bubbles, you may have a leak.

text.content: Check the air pressure

rich_text.plain_text: Check the air pressure

text.content: Make sure the air pressure is at the recommended level.

rich_text.plain_text: Make sure the air pressure is at the recommended level.

text.content: V. Proper operator training

rich_text.plain_text: V. Proper operator training

text.content: Having a good operator training is essential to not only outlasting your construction equipment, but also your tires. When conducting your operator training, consider the following attributes:

rich_text.plain_text: Having a good operator training is essential to not only outlasting your construction equipment, but also your tires. When conducting your operator training, consider the following attributes:

text.content: Avoid these moments: unnecessary backing up, spinning of tires, erratic movements.

rich_text.plain_text: Avoid these moments: unnecessary backing up, spinning of tires, erratic movements.

text.content: Know your best driving path: be aware of the layout in the working area and carefully plan their routes. How steep are the slopes? Are there areas or deep mud or hard-to-spot hollows? Going around an obstacle or mound or steep incline may take a little longer, but the savings in fuel and tire damage may well make it worthwhile.

rich_text.plain_text: Know your best driving path: be aware of the layout in the working area and carefully plan their routes. How steep are the slopes? Are there areas or deep mud or hard-to-spot hollows? Going around an obstacle or mound or steep incline may take a little longer, but the savings in fuel and tire damage may well make it worthwhile.

text.content: In summary, if you abide to the top five tips we have mentioned above in maintaining your construction equipment tires, you will notice a significant difference in maximizing your operation cost-per-hour and having the best productivity.
